title: Patient safety surgicial pads and methods of making the same
abstract: Patient safety surgical pads are disclosed. Methods of making and using patient safety surgical pads are also disclosed.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A patient safety surgical pad comprising: a fiber-containing layer having an upper fiber-containing layer surface and a lower fiber-containing layer surface; a high coefficient of friction layer along the lower fiber-containing layer surface; and a foam layer along the upper fiber-containing layer surface. 2. The patient safety surgical pad of claim 1, wherein the fiber-containing layer comprises one or more nonwoven layers, one or more woven layers, one or more knit layers, one or more unidirectional fiber layers, or any combination thereof. 3. The patient safety surgical pad of claim 1 or 2, wherein the fiber-containing layer comprises one or more nonwoven layers. 4. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the fiber-containing layer comprises a spunbonded nonwoven or a spunbonded/meltblown/spunbonded (SMS) nonwoven. 5. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the fiber-containing layer comprises at least one layer of a spunbonded nonwoven comprising polypropylene fibers. 6. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the fiber-containing layer has a layer thickness trcL of from about 0.5 millimeter (mm) to about 3.0 mm. 7. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the high coefficient of friction layer comprises a polymeric layer. 8. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the high coefficient of friction layer comprises polyvinyl chloride. 9. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the high coefficient of friction layer comprises a textured outermost surface having undulations along the outermost surface. 10. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 7 to 9, wherein the high coefficient of friction layer further comprises a fibrous reinforcement within or bonded to said polymeric layer. 11. The patient safety surgical pad of claim 10, wherein the fibrous reinforcement comprises a polyester knit fabric calendared within said polymeric layer, said polyester knit fabric having a warp tear strength of about 2.5 kg and a weft tear strength of about 1.8 kg as measured by ASTM D751 test method. 12. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 11, wherein the high coefficient of friction layer has a layer thickness tHL of from about 0.3 mm to about 1.0 mm. 13. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the high coefficient of friction layer has a layer thickness tHL of about 0.8 mm. 14. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the foam layer comprises a single foam layer. 15. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the foam layer comprises two or more foam layers in combination with one another. 16. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 13 and 15, wherein the foam layer comprises two foam layers in combination with one another. 17. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 16, wherein the foam layer has a layer density of from about 0.5 to about 5.0 lb density. 18. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 17, wherein the foam layer has a layer density of from about 2.5 to about 4.0 lb density. 19. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the foam layer has an Impression Load Deflection (ILD) value of from about 36 to about 50. 20. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein the foam layer has an Impression Load Deflection (ILD) value of from about 40 to about 50. 21. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the foam layer has a layer thickness troL of from about 1.0 inches (in) to about 4.0 in. 22. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 21, wherein the foam layer has a layer thickness troL of from about 1.0 in to about 2.0 in. 23. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 22, wherein the foam layer comprises a polyurethane foam, a polyester foam, a polyester polyurethane foam, or a combination thereof. 24. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 23, wherein the foam layer comprises a polyurethane non-viscoelastic foam, a polyester non-viscoelastic foam, a polyester polyurethane non-viscoelastic foam, or a combination thereof. 25. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 13 and 15 to 24, wherein the foam layer comprises (i) an upper foam layer having an upper foam layer thickness of from about 0.25 in to about 1.0 in, and (ii) a lower foam layer having a lower foam layer thickness of from about 0.5 in to about 1.5 in. 26. The patient safety surgical pad 10 of embodiment 25, wherein (i) the upper foam layer has a high tack coefficient of at least 1.0 as measured via ASTM test method D1894, and (ii) the lower foam layer has an average breathability of at least about 3.0 standard cubic feet per minute (SCFM) as measured via ASTM 3574 Test G. 27. The patient safety surgical pad of claim 25 or 26, wherein (i) the upper foam layer has an Impression Load Deflection (ILD) value of about 50, and (ii) the lower foam layer has an 

 Impression Load Deflection (ILD) value of about 40. 28. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 25 to 27, wherein (i) the upper foam layer comprises a polyester polyurethane non-viscoelastic foam, and (ii) the lower foam layer comprises a polyurethane non-viscoelastic foam. 29. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 28, further comprising a first adhesive layer between the high coefficient of friction layer and the lower fiber-containing layer surface of the fiber-containing layer. 30. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 29, further comprising a second adhesive layer between the foam layer and the upper fiber-containing layer surface of the fiber- containing layer. 31. The patient safety surgical pad of claim 30, wherein the second adhesive layer comprises a patterned adhesive layer that covers less than an entire area between the foam layer and the upper fiber-containing layer surface of the fiber-containing layer. 32. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 31, further comprising stitching so as to secure two or more layers to one another. 33. The patient safety surgical pad of claim 32, wherein the stitching secures two foam layers to one another. 34. The patient safety surgical pad of claim 32 or 33, wherein the stitching secures one or more foam layers to the fiber-containing layer. 35. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 32 to 34, wherein the stitching secures one or more foam layers to the fiber-containing layer and the friction layer. 36. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 32 to 35, wherein the stitching extends along at least one edge of the foam layer. 37. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 32 to 36, wherein the stitching extends along at least two edges of the foam layer. 38. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 32 to 37, wherein the stitching extends along each edge of the foam layer. 39. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 38, further comprising one or more handles positioned along opposite side edges of the patient safety surgical pad. 40. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 39, further comprising one or more handles positioned along opposite side edges of the patient safety surgical pad and within the fiber-containing layer. 41. The patient safety surgical pad of claim 39 or 40, wherein each handle may independently have a desired shape and size. 42. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 41, wherein the patient safety surgical pad is capable of supporting and/or transporting and/or enabling transfer of a patient having a patient weight of up to about 400 kilograms (kg) (i.e., about 880 pounds (lbs)). 43. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 42, wherein the patient safety surgical pad has an overall width Wo of from about 16 in to about 40 in, and an overall length L 0 of from about 24 in to about 60 in. 44. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 43, wherein the patient safety surgical pad has an overall width Wo about 33 in, and an overall length L 0 of about 40 in. 45. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 44, wherein the foam layer has an overall width WFOL that is less than an overall width WFCL of the fiber-containing layer. 46. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 45, wherein the high coefficient of friction layer has an overall width WHL that is less than an overall width WFCL of the fiber- containing layer. 47. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 46, wherein the high coefficient of friction layer has a width WHL that is greater than an overall width WFOL of foam layer. 48. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 47, wherein the high coefficient of friction layer has a length LHL that is less than an overall length Lo of the fiber-containing layer. 49. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 48, wherein the high coefficient of friction layer has a length LHL that is from about 8 to about 12 in less than an overall length Lo of the fiber-containing layer. 50. The patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 49, wherein a lower surface portion of the lower fiber-containing layer surface is not covered by the high coefficient of friction layer. 51. The patient safety surgical pad of claim 50, wherein the lower surface portion is positioned along a head end, along a foot end, or both. 52. A method of making the patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 51, said method comprising: attaching the high coefficient of friction layer along the lower fiber-containing layer surface of the fiber-containing layer; and attaching the foam layer along the upper fiber-containing layer surface of the fiber- containing layer. 53. The method of claim 52, wherein the foam layer comprises two or more foam layers, and the method further comprises: combining the two or more foam layers with one another. 54. The method of claim 52 or 53, wherein each of said attaching steps comprises utilizing an adhesive to attach the high coefficient of friction layer and the foam layer to the fiber-containing layer. 55. The method of any one of claims 52 to 54, further comprising: stitching one or more layers to one another via stitching. 56. The method of any one of claims 52 to 55, wherein the step of attaching the high coefficient of friction layer along the lower fiber-containing layer surface of the fiber-containing layer comprises: attaching a high coefficient of friction layer that has a length LHL that is less than an overall length Lo of the fiber-containing layer. 57. The method of any one of claims 52 to 56, wherein a lower surface portion of the lower fiber-containing layer surface is not covered by the high coefficient of friction layer. 58. A method of using the patient safety surgical pad of any one of claims 1 to 51, said method comprising: supporting a patient on the patient safety surgical pad. 59. The method of claim 58, further comprising: positioning the patient safety surgical pad along an operating room table. 60. The method of claim 58 or 59, further comprising: lifting a patient via the patient safety surgical pad. 61. The method of any one of claims 58 to 60, further comprising: transferring a patient from one location to another location via the patient safety surgical pad. 62. The method of any one of claims 58 to 61, further comprising: cutting thru a portion of the patient safety surgical pad. 63. The method of any one of claims 58 to 62, further comprising: cutting thru the lower surface portion of the patient safety surgical pad.
